text:
Once upon a twilight in the quaint village of Willowbrook, there lived a peculiar old woman named Mildred. Mildred was always seen wearing a beret atop her head and carrying an antique tin flute. She lived in a cottage frayed at the edges but warm with memories.

One peculiar evening, while peeling potatoes—her secret hobby—a mysterious fox stole her flute. Panicking, she began flute-less flutters through the village. Every door she knocked on replied with only silence. She searched everywhere but couldn't find her beloved instrument.

In desperation, Mildred sought the village sage's wisdom. The sage was wise beyond his years, seldom seen but always there, his beard a tapestry of stories. He had never heard about the flute thief but knew how lonely old lady birds could become.

He offered her a plan like none other. Every Saturday night, at the stroke of eight, she would play the flute under the full moon. The tune would waft through the forest, a
